definitions: interface = a point where two things meet and interact with each other. so, like "bridge" or "connector" between two different things that lets them communicate
api is the interface part of the backend that frontend talks to
api is written inside backend code; it is a backend(server + logic + database + api + etc etc etc) component

another way to define it would be, a way for two programs to talk to each other(exchange data/information); the "two programs" can be any two separate pieces of software that need to communicate. for example, your extension + your backend, your backend + OpenAI, weather app + weather service, your landing page + your backend. we are calling them two different programs cause, 1)they’ve separate codebases (different files, different locations) and 2) they’re running independently(like extension runs in browser, backend runs on server)
note: it can be of the following types;

types.name: backend api
types.description: you build your own in your server for connecting with client/frontend, like we would in node.js

types.name: external api
types.description: someone else's backend, like you call with openai’s ai, or with some weather showing server website, map showing server website

types.name: browser api(frontened apis)
types.description: built into browser to connect with servers; like fetch(), localStorage()

definitions: asking another service for data
the instructions you write that create programs
basically, the text you write (in a programming language) to give instructions to the computer. when executed, this code becomes a program
code is static (just text sitting in files) but when you run/execute it, it becomes a program (doing things)
the instructions you write that create programs
basically, the text you write (in a programming language) to give instructions to the computer. when executed, this code becomes a program
code is static (just text sitting in files) but when you run/execute it, it becomes a program (doing things)
the complete collection of all source code files for a project or program
basically, all the code files that make up your project, stored in one place (all the code files for your project)
remember- codebase are the actual code files themselves (the content) and file structure is how those files are organized - the folder/file arrangement (the organization)
putting project online for others to use
secret config (api keys, passwords)
how the code files are organized into folders and subfolders
how the code files are organized into folders and subfolders
complete skeleton/structure for building applications that enforces specific patterns and controls the overall flow of your program
inversion of control — the framework calls YOUR code, not the other way around
opinionated — has strong rules about how you should organize and write code
comprehensive — provides the entire foundation, not just individual tools
dictates architecture — you work within its structure

types.name: node.js
types.description: express.js - web server, next.js - full-stack React apps, nestJS - enterprise applications

types.name: python
types.description: django - full-stack web framework, flask - lightweight web framework, fastAPI - modern API framework

types.name: frontend
types.description: react - UI framework, vue - UI framework, angular - UI framework

definitions: frontend + backend
the rules/language browsers and servers use to talk to each other
protocol used between client & server
there are 9 standard http methods:

types.name: get
types.description: retrieve/read data; asking server to send you information

types.name: post
types.description: create/send new data; sending data to server to create something new

types.name: put
types.description: update/Replace entire resource; replacing all data of something with new data

types.name: patch
types.description: partially update resource; updating just part of the data

types.name: delete
types.description: remove resource; remove/delete data from server

types.name: head
types.description: same as get but only retrieves headers (no body); used to check if a resource exists without downloading it

types.name: options
types.description: ask server what methods are allowed; checks CORS (cross origin resource sharing)

types.name: connect
types.description: establish a tunnel (for proxies); used for very specific networking scenarios

types.name: trace
types.description: echo back the request (for debugging); rarely used, mostly for troubleshooting

definitions: a format for organizing and sending data (looks like javascript objects)
basically, a universal language that both frontend and backend understand
collection of pre-written modules that solve common problems; someone else already wrote the code, tested it, and packaged it for you to use
you're in control — you decide when and how to use it
passive — it sits there waiting for you to call it
modular — you can use just the parts you need
specific purpose — solves a particular category of problems

types.name: node.js
types.description: axios - HTTP requests, mysql2 - database connections, moment - date manipulation, bcrypt - password hashing

types.name: python
types.description: requests - HTTP requests (Python's axios), pandas - data analysis, numpy - numerical computing, matplotlib - plotting/graphs, beautifulsoup - web scraping

definitions: your own computer as a server
single, focused piece of code that does ONE specific thing, think of it as the smallest building block
single responsibility — does one job only
self-contained — all the code for that one job is in one place
reusable — you can use it anywhere in your project
importable — you bring it into other files when needed

types.name: node.js
types.description: fs - file operations, path - file paths, os - operating system info, crypto - encryption

types.name: python
types.description: math - mathematical functions, random - random numbers, datetime - date/time handling, json - JSON parsing

types.name: your own
types.description: helpers.js / utils.py - your utility functions

definitions: bundle of code (modules/libraries/frameworks) that's been packaged up and published for others to download and use
code you write → package it up → publish to npm → others can install it
note: npm (node package manager) is like an app store for JavaScript code. installing via: javascript/node.js: npm, yarn, pnpm — python: pip, conda

definitions: door number server listens on (like 3000, 5000)
a set of instructions that tell the computer what to do.
running/executing a program: the process of the computer following the instructions given to it step-by-step

types.name: applications (apps)
types.description: programs you use directly; example- browser, instagram, browser extension

types.name: system software
types.description: programs that run the computer itself; example- windows, macOS, linux

types.name: scripts/code
types.description: small programs that do specific tasks; example- javascript file that checks urls, python script that analyzes data, your backend server code

definitions: what the client (frontend) sends to the server (backend) asking for something
communication between client & server
what the server sends back to the client after processing the request.
communication between client & server

parts.name: status code
parts.description: did it work? (200 = success, 404 = not found, 500 = error)

example:
Status: 200 OK
Body: { "safe": true, "reason": "No threats detected" }

definitions: the complete ecosystem needed to run your code (includes interpreter/compiler + libraries + APIs + memory management + everything else)

parts.name: apis to interact with system
parts.description: lets your code talk to the OS

types.name: node.js runtime
types.description: includes V8 interpreter + Node APIs + event loop

types.name: python runtime
types.description: includes Python interpreter + standard library

types.name: JVM
types.description: Java Virtual Machine - includes Java compiler + runtime libraries

note: runtime environment CONTAINS the interpreter/compiler + extras. interpreter = simultaneous translator (listens and translates sentence-by-sentence in real-time). compiler = book translator (reads entire book, translates it all, then publishes). runtime environment = the entire conference setup (room + translator + microphone + seats + everything needed for the event to happen)

definitions: programs and instructions that tell a computer what to do. the non-physical part of a computer.
note: software is a general term for all programs, program is a specific piece of software, application(app) is software you use directly. (software is a group of multiple programs. though, a single program can be called software too)

definitions: the combination of technologies/tools you'll use to build the project.
software used during development to write, run, test, and manage code

types.name: node.js
types.description: javaScript runtime — lets you run JavaScript outside browser

types.name: npm
types.description: package manager — installs libraries/tools (comes with node.js)

types.name: vs code
types.description: code editor

types.name: git
types.description: version control — saves your code history, teamwork

types.name: github
types.description: code hosting — store code online, share with team

types.name: postman
types.description: api tester — test your backend without building frontend yet

types.name: mysql workbench
types.description: database viewer — see your database visually

types.name: chrome devtools
types.description: browser debugger — see errors, test code in browser

types.name: terminal/command prompt
types.description: command line — run commands to start servers, install things

definitions: the address/location of something on the internet
note: path of a url: the part of the URL that comes AFTER the domain name, specifying which specific resource/page you want. example: url path = /check-url
